type: Affiliate coupon site (AliExpress via Admitad, WordPress)
one_line_reason: PASS: a single-program AliExpress affiliate/SEO coupon site whose only growth lever (content + SEO + audience work) is exactly this team's weakness — growth_lever_fits fails; 100% revenue-concentrated in one clawback-prone program, neglected/likely declining, in a category under attribution assault (Honey/extension hijacking). Cheap and cash-flowing but over the $3k budget — Pass on merits, so the budget flag is moot.

operability_upside.note: WordPress coupon site is technically runnable by the team, but it's a content/SEO treadmill — the value-add is editorial, not engineering; coupon-import automation is trivial and not the revenue driver, so product/eng upside is low.

pmf_retention.note: Transient coupon-seekers with no loyalty (one-time-purchase stickiness); a 6,000+ Telegram audience is a mild plus but unverified; seller admits neglect/likely decline. Weak/unknown retention.

unit_economics.note: ~$245/mo net on ~$300 gross, but 100% from one affiliate program (AliExpress/Admitad), clawback-prone and seasonal (11.11 spikes); unverified (Jetpack-only). Thin/erratic ceiling.

moat_defensibility.note: Commodity coupon niche vs RetailMeNot/Honey/extensions; single SEO channel + single affiliate program; last-click attribution being hijacked industry-wide (Honey/extension scandal). Nothing durable to defend.

growth_market.note: Claimed growth paths (Temu/SHEIN, display ads, Telegram) are all marketing work; category under structural pressure (Honey/Awin suspension, Google 2025 extension policy). Slowly declining.

tech_ip.note: WordPress, simple and transferable; content is coupon listings (low IP). Affiliate-account/link transfer is uncertain and Jetpack-only stats weaken verification — workable with real dependence.

transfer_key_person.note: SEO/domain distribution is impersonal and transfers, but 100% of revenue rides an AliExpress/Admitad affiliate account that may not transfer, and Telegram ownership transfer is unconfirmed — much of the realized value could walk.

deal_terms.note: $4,900 BIN ≈ 20x monthly / ~1.7x annual net = cheapish/below-market, but all-cash with no seller financing, and above the $3k ceiling (fair-ish but rigid and a strain).

seller_adverse_selection.note: Neglected several months (likely cleaned-up decline), ~100% revenue concentration in one program, seasonal 'average', Jetpack-only stats, selling on Reddit + Flippa — thin story with peak-metrics timing.

key_unknowns: Real monthly earnings trend (Admitad) vs the '~$300 average' — how much is 11.11/big-sale spikes and what are the last 3 months given the neglect? Swings unit economics and adverse-selection.

Revenue concentration & transfer: is it 100% AliExpress/Admitad, and do the affiliate account and existing links transfer, or must they be re-issued under a new account (they often don't transfer)? Gates ip_transferable and value_transfers.
Telegram 6,000+ subs: organic vs bought, engagement, and whether channel ownership can actually transfer — the one potentially real off-Google asset.
Traffic trend and quality (Search Console): any Google penalty/core-update hit; how much is US vs low-monetizing geos (Russia). Verifies fragility.
Exposure to the affiliate-attribution shift (Honey/extension hijacking, Google 2025 extension policy) on AliExpress conversions.
seller_questions: Screen-share the Admitad dashboard: 12-24 months of monthly earnings, confirmed vs pending/clawed-back. Is '~$300/mo' a trailing average, and what are the last 3 months? (unit economics + adverse selection; verifies revenue and decline)
How seasonal is it — what share of revenue is AliExpress big-sale periods (11.11, etc.) vs baseline months? (revenue quality)
Search Console + Jetpack: 12-month traffic trend, top pages/queries, any manual penalty or core-update drop, and AI Overview presence on coupon queries? (fragility)
Is revenue 100% AliExpress/Admitad? On sale, does the affiliate account transfer, or do I re-apply and re-issue every link (and does AliExpress permit it)? (concentration + transferability)
Telegram 6,000+ subs: how acquired (organic vs paid), current engagement/opens, any revenue today, and can channel ownership actually transfer to me? (asset verification)
Why sell at $4,900 now, how long has it been neglected, other bidders? Any trademark/policy risk using 'AliExpress' branding, or content that's AI-generated/duplicated? (adverse selection + legal)
inputs_provided: Listing: AliExpress coupon affiliate site, WordPress, country coupon pages + blog [VERIFIED — site fetched: country pages, affiliate redirect links, Site Kit]
Monetized exclusively via AliExpress affiliate through Admitad [seller-stated, consistent with site]
Geo: US, then Russia, Italy, Germany, France, UK [seller-stated]
Reason: lack of time; neglected several months; 'was growing when active' [seller-stated -> decline signal]
Uses Jetpack Stats, not Google Analytics [seller-stated -> weaker verification]

scorecard_markdown:
## Acquisition Scorecard — AlixCadet.com (v2)

**Verdict: PASS — 41/100 merit** (completeness ~54%). Over the $3,000 budget ($4,900 all-cash, no financing), but a Pass on merits so the budget flag is suppressed.

Single-program AliExpress affiliate/SEO coupon site (WordPress). The only growth lever is content + SEO + audience work — exactly this team's weakness — so **growth_lever_fits fails** and the deal caps at PASS. 100% revenue concentration in one clawback-prone affiliate program, neglected/likely declining, category under attribution assault (Honey/extension hijacking).

### Dimensions
- Operability & upside: 2/5 (med) — WordPress runnable, but content/SEO treadmill; near-zero engineering upside.
- PMF & retention: 2/5 (low) — transient coupon-seekers, no loyalty; Telegram a mild plus; neglected/declining.
- Unit economics: 2/5 (low) — ~$245/mo net, 100% one affiliate program, clawback-prone, seasonal, unverified.
- Moat & defensibility: 1/5 (high) — commodity niche vs RetailMeNot/Honey; single SEO channel + single program; attribution being hijacked.
- Growth & market: 2/5 (med) — claimed paths all marketing work; category under structural pressure.
- Tech & IP: 3/5 (med) — WordPress transferable, low IP; affiliate-account transfer uncertain; Jetpack-only stats.
- Transfer / key-person: 2/5 (low) — SEO/domain transfers, but 100%-revenue affiliate account and Telegram may not.
- Deal terms: 3/5 (med) — ~1.7x annual net, cheapish, but all-cash and over budget.
- Seller / adverse-selection: 2/5 (med) — neglect + concentration + seasonal 'average' + Jetpack-only + Reddit/Flippa sale.

### Gates
- operable: **true** · growth_lever_fits: **false (kill → PASS)** · ip_transferable: unknown · value_transfers: unknown · not_fragile: **false** (100% single-program concentration = structural SPOF) · revenue_evidence: **seller_claimed**.

### Key unknowns
Earnings trend/seasonality (Admitad); revenue concentration + affiliate-account/link transferability; Telegram authenticity & transfer; traffic trend/penalty (Search Console); exposure to the affiliate-attribution shift.
